rootCause: Operational, not code. ERR_CONNECTION_REFUSED in M637F means no process was listening on 127.0.0.1:8787 at the moment of the QA test, so the WebSocket upgrade never reached the bridge. When the bridge process is actually started, it binds 127.0.0.1:8787 and serves /health, /runtime, /debug and the /ws/extension upgrade correctly. The reconnect loop in M637F was caused by the bridge not running, not by the extension or the bridge protocol.
errConnectionRefusedExplainedBy: No TCP listener on 127.0.0.1:8787 during the test (bridge process not started or already exited). ERR_CONNECTION_REFUSED with no invalid_token, no close 1008 and no CSP violation is the textbook signature of a missing listener, not a protocol/auth/CSP failure.
liveProofMethod: Started the bridge locally (dotnet run --project src/OneBrain.ChromeLab.Bridge), then probed every endpoint and the WebSocket upgrade with real requests, captured the listening PID/address, then stopped the test process and confirmed the port was freed.
operationalConclusion: The bridge must be started explicitly by the operator; the extension cannot auto-start it. M637G delivers a read-only liveness diagnostic script and a startup runbook so this blocker is self-diagnosable and not repeated.
